seoTitle: How to Add a Visa Gift Card to PayPal (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to register and link a prepaid Visa gift card inside your PayPal wallet so you can use it as a funding source for purchases.
intro: Prepaid Visa gift cards don't come with a default billing address, which can trigger fraud flags if you try to use them online without registering first. This guide walks through registering your card and linking it inside PayPal. It's for anyone who wants to spend a gift card balance through PayPal checkouts.

steps.heading: Register the card's billing address
steps.body: Use the registration link printed on the back of your Visa gift card to map your home address to the card.

steps.heading: Open your PayPal wallet
steps.body: Log in to PayPal and move to the Wallet view to see your currently linked instruments.

steps.heading: Link the card
steps.body: Click Link a Card, select Debit/Prepaid, and enter the 16-digit number, expiration date, and CVV.

steps.heading: Match the billing address
steps.body: Confirm the billing address dropdown matches exactly what you registered with the card issuer.

steps.heading: Use it as a funding source
steps.body: Click Link Card to authorize, then select the gift card row as your funding source the next time you send money or check out.

faq.question: Why did my gift card get declined?
faq.answer: A mismatched billing address or skipping registration are the most common reasons for a Card Declined error.

faq.question: Can I combine a gift card with another funding source?
faq.answer: Yes, if the gift card balance doesn't cover the full amount, you can pair it with a secondary backup card.

faq.question: Does linking the card cost anything?
faq.answer: There's typically no fee to link a card, though standard transaction fees may apply when you use it.

faq.question: Can I remove the gift card once the balance is used up?
faq.answer: Yes, you can unlink a card from your wallet at any time once it's no longer needed.
